body.title: Add foreground flag to InferenceRequest for cloud-preferring routing
body.result: foreground=True field on InferenceRequest; rules_engine sort flips to cloud-first when set; ToolLoop + _chat_inference pass it; 3 new tests in test_flat_rate_routing.py all green (232 total passing)
body.intention: I intend that coding/foreground calls from DickSimnel always route to cloud models (Claude via OR) rather than flat-rate Ollama, since sprint-ticket work is latency-sensitive and requires high capability.
body.description:
InferenceRequest has no foreground flag. The rules engine currently prefers flat-rate (Ollama Pro) over usage-based (OpenRouter) for all task classes. This means DickSimnel coding calls can silently route to local Ollama instead of cloud Claude. Adding foreground=True on InferenceRequest and honoring it in the rules engine sort order (cloud-first when foreground) fixes this.

Design open question left for CC review: keep Dick's _TIER_CASCADE manually looping in device.py, or migrate it into the proxy as escalation_hop logic. This ticket only covers the foreground flag + rules engine sort. Cascade migration is explicitly out of scope.

**Affected files:** devices/inference/shim.py (InferenceRequest dataclass), devices/inference/rules_engine.py (candidate sort), devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py (passes foreground=True), devices/dicksimnel/device.py (_chat_inference passes foreground=True)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; interface logging (InferenceRequest is a bus envelope — log the new flag at dispatch)
**Scope boundary:** IN: foreground field on InferenceRequest, rules_engine sort flips to cloud-first when foreground=True, ToolLoop + Dick chat pass it. OUT: cascade migration into proxy, any change to _TIER_CASCADE logic, budget gates.
**Completion criteria:** (1) InferenceRequest has foreground: bool = False field; (2) rules_engine.route() accepts foreground kwarg and when True sorts usage_based before flat_rate; (3) ToolLoop builds every InferenceRequest with foreground=True; (4) tests: test_inference.py covers foreground=True routes to usage_based source before flat_rate when both available; (5) existing tests stay green.

## Escalation summary
**What was tried:** MAX_TURNS: hit 20 turns without DONE: prefix
**Where it broke:** max turns hit without completing
**What now?**
